page_title: The 10 Best Happy Hours in Houston Right Now

restaurants.name: Angel Share
restaurants.image_alt: Modern industrial restaurant interior with circular bar, skylight, and shuffleboard.
restaurants.blurb: $5 cocktails, $5 wells, $5 wine by the glass, $5 drafts, six days a week 4 to 7pm. There is no other Houston cocktail bar running this much value across every category at once, and the 4.8 rating tells you the bartending hasn't slipped.

deals.details:
$5 Off Wine By The Glass
$5 Select Cocktails
$5 Wells
$5 Drafts

restaurants.name: Lost & Found
restaurants.image_alt: Overhead view of a well-plated steak dinner with sides.
restaurants.blurb: Iconic Fourth Ward bar with a 4.8 rating across 17,000-plus reviews. $5 premium drinks, $5 wines, $5 frozens four days a week, then 75-cent wings on Saturdays. The Houston spot you take out-of-towners to prove the city has a bar scene.

items: Same happy hour menu runs late on Fridays

deals.details:
$5 Premium Drinks
$5 Frozens
$5 Wine
75 Cent Wings

$5 Premium Drinks
$5 Frozens
$5 Wine
$30 - 10 Oz Steak & Cajun Green Beans

restaurants.name: Pistolero's
restaurants.cuisine: Mexican, Bar And Grill, Bar, Restaurant
restaurants.image_alt: Overhead shot of a colorful salad, nachos, and drinks on a table.
restaurants.deal_headline: $4 House Margs Daily, $5 Milagros & $7 Apps Mon-Fri
restaurants.blurb: $4 house margaritas every single day, then a Mon-Fri 4 to 7pm window stacks on $5 Centinela margs, $4 sangrias, $1 off drafts, and $7 elote, taquitos, tamales, queso. The Westheimer Mexican joint that keeps the whole Montrose afternoon cheap.

items: $7 apps: elote, taquitos, tamales, chicken quesadillas, nachos, stuffed jalapeños

restaurants.name: Black Walnut Kitchen & Bar
restaurants.cuisine: American, Brunch, Breakfast, Coffee Shop, Cafe, Restaurant
restaurants.image_alt: Martini and glazed pork belly skewers on a white plate.
restaurants.deal_headline: $5 Wine, Wells & Drafts Twice Daily, Mon-Fri
restaurants.blurb: $5 house wine, $5 wells, $5 draft beer, with happy hour running twice a day: morning 7 to 9:30am for the breakfast-meeting crowd and afternoon 3 to 6:30pm for everyone else. Rice Village staple that takes the day-drinking long view.

items: Same $5 program runs at breakfast hours

restaurants.name: Bungalow
restaurants.image_alt: Close-up: Golden-yellow cocktail with gold leaf and chili garnish.
restaurants.blurb: $1 raw oysters, $3 chargrilled, $6 select wines, $9 cocktails, plus $8 wagyu meatballs and $10 seafood dip. Mon to Thu 3 to 7pm at the Main Street steakhouse-and-oyster bar. The deepest Gulf-Coast oyster discount in downtown.

items: $10 seafood dip and pineapple-glazed pork belly
Same happy hour menu runs an hour shorter on Fridays

restaurants.name: Social Beer Garden HTX
restaurants.image_alt: Hand holding amber beverage with foam against blurred bar background.
restaurants.deal_headline: $1 Off Beer, $4.40 Wells, Plus Reverse Happy Hour
restaurants.blurb: $1 off every beer (cans and drafts), $4 off signature cocktails, $4.40 wells, $5.50 frozens, $5.50 house margs Mon to Thu 3 to 8pm. Then a reverse happy hour 7 to 11pm same nights. Midtown's defining outdoor drinking patio.

items: $5.50 Benchmark Bonded Bourbon, Jose Cuervo Traditional
$4.40 Friday menu: draft of the week, green tea shots, house marg, lemon drops, wells

restaurants.name: Dean's Downtown
restaurants.image_alt: Cocktail with a foamy top, dried citrus, straw, and purple flowers.
restaurants.blurb: Seven days a week, 5 to 8pm: $5 Old Fashioneds, $5 Tom Collins, $5 house liquor, $6 Greyhounds, $7 Moscow Mules, $7 Daiquiris, $2 off all whiskey. The Main Street cocktail bar that runs the longest reliable happy hour window downtown.

items: $5 House Old Fashioned and Tom Collins
$7 Moscow Mule, Daiquiri, Tequila Rico, Dean's Ranch Water

restaurants.name: Postino Montrose
restaurants.image_alt: A rustic cheese and fruit plate with flatbread crisps, brie, walnuts, and dried citrus for a light, shareable bite.
restaurants.deal_headline: $6 Wine, $6 Beer Pitchers, Plus $25 Board-and-Bottle Nights
restaurants.blurb: $6 glasses of wine and $6 beer pitchers Mon to Fri 11am to 5pm at the Montrose wine bar, then Mon-Tue evenings run a $25 board-of-bruschetta-plus-bottle-of-wine deal. The afternoon wine option Houston actually needs.

items: Same $6 wine and beer-pitcher menu, weekend brunch hours
$25 board of bruschetta plus bottle of wine

restaurants.name: PKL Social
restaurants.image_alt: Overhead view of a wooden table with various breakfast and brunch plates.
restaurants.blurb: $5 draft beers, $6 house wines, $7 classic and frozen cocktails, 25% off snacks Mon to Thu 3 to 6:30pm. Plus a Tuesday $5 burger night. The Shepherd Drive pickleball-and-bar concept turned out to have one of the better happy hours in the Memorial corridor.

items: $5 single FM Burger or smash burger

restaurants.name: The Cellar Bar
restaurants.image_alt: Interior of a dimly lit bar with a fully stocked bar.
restaurants.deal_headline: $3 Domestic Beer, $3.50 Drafts, Six Days a Week
restaurants.blurb: $3 domestic beer, $3.50 drafts, $4 import bottles, $5 Titos and Jack and Bacardi, $5 house wines. Six days a week 2 to 7pm on Richmond Ave. The Upper Kirby dive that keeps Houston honest about cheap drinks.
